channel.title: Salad on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Salad on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Zesty Asian Chicken Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Isabelle Dorval

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup rice vinegar
  • 8 tsp reduced sodium soy sauce
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 tbsp cilantro, chopped
  • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, minced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1/2 tsp sesame oil
  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
  • 4-3 oz boned and skinned chicken breast halves
  • 2 cups broccoli florets
  • 2 carrots, cut into 1 1/2 inch long matchsticks
  • 1 cup (6 ozs.) pitted prunes, snipped into halves
  • 3 green onions, sliced
  • 4 lettuce leaves

Instructions

To make dressing/marinade, in medium bowl whisk vinegar, soy, cilantro, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and pepper flakes. Remove 1/4 cup of mixture into a shallow 8 inch baking dish. Place chicken breasts in baking dish. Turn to coat both sides. Let chicken marinate for 30 minutes.

item.title: Tomato Soup Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Emily and Robert Desbois

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 1 lemon Jello

  • 1 can tomato soup

  • 1 small cream cheese

  • 3 sticks celery, chopped

  • 1 medium onion, chopped

  • ¾ cup almonds, chopped

  • 1 cup Miracle Whip

Instructions

Heat Jello, soup and cream cheese until melted. Add other ingredients. Put green peppers on top if desired.

Place in fridge. Make a day ahead.

item.title: Spinach Salad with Strawberries
item.description:
Submitted by Patti and Darren Henri

Cousins of the Bride

  • Spinach, washed and torn
  • 1 pint of fresh strawberries, washed and sliced
  • ½ cup slivered almonds

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ to ½ cup of sugar
  • 2 tbsp sesame seeds
  • 1 tbsp poppy seeds
  • ¼ tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 1 ½ tsp chopped onion
  • ¼ tsp paprika
  • ½ cup vegetable oil
  • ¼ cup cider vinegar

Instructions

Make the dressing the day before. Pour over salad, toss and serve.

item.title: Salade d'Amour
item.description:
Submitted by Rachel and Wayne Pigeau

Godparents/Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

  • 2 cups cooked rice, cooled
  • 1 ½ cup fresh, chopped spinach
  • 3 stalks celery, cut diagonally
  • ¼ cup raisins
  • ½ cup green peppers, chopped
  • ½ cup red peppers, chopped
  • 3 sprigs fresh parsley
  • ½ cup green onions, chopped
  • 1 cup fresh, sliced mushrooms
  • 2 cups fresh (not canned) bean sprouts
  • ½ cup cashews

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup soya sauce
  • ½ cup oil
  • 2 garlic buds (or more if you like)

Instructions

Put dressing in a jar and add raisins. Let stand overnight in refrigerator. Mix all ingredients together and pour dressing. Let sit in refrigerator for one hour before serving.

item.title: Rice Greek Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Lynn Sayer

Cousin of the Bride

  • 2 cups cooked and cooled, Uncle Ben’s Converted Brand rice
  • 3/4 cup English cucumber, cut into slices
  • 3/4 cup tomatoes, diced
  • 3/4 cup green and red peppers, diced
  • 1/3 cup red onion, diced
  • 1 cup Feta cheese, cut into large dice
  • 1/3 cup Kalamata olives, pitted

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup olive oil
  • 1/4 cup red wine vinegar
  • 1 garlic, minced (about 1 tsp)
  • 1 tsp rubbed oregano
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp pepper

Instructions

In a large bowl, combine cooked rice with all diced vegetables, diced cheese and olives.

item.title: Party Lime Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Deanna and Larry Shank

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 can crushed pineapple
  • 1 small package of lime Jello
  • 1 cup whipping cream
  • 1 cup chopped celery
  • 6 ozs cream cheese, room temperature
  • 1 cup chopped nuts

Instructions

Heat pineapple. Dissolve lime Jello in pineapple. Stir well. Set in refrigerator until partially set.

Whip cream. Beat in cream cheese. Add nuts and celery. Add cream mixture to pineapple mixture.

item.title: Pasta Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup oil
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 2 ½ tbsp sugar
  • ¼ cup concentrated orange juice
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 tsp crushed red pepper
  • ½ tsp basil
  • black pepper
  • salt

Instructions

Cook choice of pasta, drain and add the dressing. Add crushed feta cheese, parmesan cheese, grapes (red and green), red and green peppers, olives and celery.

Chill and serve.

item.title: Lovely Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Ellie and Mike Bell

Cousins of the Groom

  • 1 green pepper
  • 1 red pepper
  • 2 green onions
  • 2 celery stalks
  • ¼ lb mushrooms
  • ¼ lb spinach (1 bag)
  • 1 cup cooked rice
  • 4 ozs cashew nuts, optional
  • 1 tbsp fresh parsley

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ½ cup cooking oil
  • ¼ cup soya sauce
  • 1 garlic clove, minced

Instructions

Marinate dressing for one hour before using. Add to above salad 5-10 minutes before serving.

item.title: Easy Taco Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Sandra and Steve Pigeau

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 lb ground beef, browned
  • 1 large bag of Doritos, crushed into small pieces
  • 2 cups grated cheddar cheese
  • 1 medium onion, diced
  • 1 green pepper, diced
  • 1 tomato, diced
  • 1 small head of lettuce, chopped
  • 1 bottle of Kraft Catalina dressing

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. If done ahead, do not add the Doritos or dressing until served.

item.title: Broccoli Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Tracey and Pierre Beaudry

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride

  • 1/2 cup raisins
  • 1/2 cup shelled sunflower seeds
  • 1 medium red onion, chopped
  • 1/2 lb crisp, crumbled bacon
  • 3 heads broccoli florets

Combine the above ingredients in a large bowl.

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup Miracle Whip
  • 2 tsp rice vinegar
  • 1/3 cup white sugar

Instructions

Combine and add to broccoli mixture. Stir well and chill.

item.title: Best Damn Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Julie and Dave Pigeau

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 head of lettuce, chopped
  • 3 ribs of celery, chopped
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • 2 cups partially thawed peas
  • 2 cups Hellman’s (must be Hellman’s mayonnaise)
  • 2 tbsp white sugar
  • ¼ cup parmesan cheese
  • bacon bits

Instructions

In a rectangle pan, layer all ingredients beginning with lettuce, celery, onions, and peas. Spread mayonnaise over top. Sprinkle with sugar, then parmesan cheese. Put in the fridge for 24 hours. Sprinkle with bacon bits just before serving.

item.title: Tarragon Potato Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/3 lbs red potatoes, unpeeled
  • 1 small clove garlic
  • ¾ tsp dried tarragon
  • ½ cup no-oil Italian dressing
  • ½ tsp dried parsley flakes
  • 2 Tbsp minced green onions
  • ½ tsp dry mustard
  • ¾ tsp lemon juice

Instructions

Cook potatoes in boiling water to cover 15 minutes or until tender. Drain well, and cool. Cut into ¼” slices, and set aside. Combine tarragon, parsley, mustard and garlic in container of an electric blender. Cover and process 30 seconds or until pureed. Add Italian dressing and process until blended. With blender running, slowly add lemon juice, and continue to process dressing mixture until smooth. Add dressing mixture to cooked potato slices; add minced green onions, and toss well to coat. Cover and chill. Yield: 8(1/2 cup) servings.

item.title: Mexican Black Bean and Corn Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can of black beans, drained and rinsed
  • 1 can of corn, drained
  • 1/2 cup of salsa (no sugar added)
  • 2 Tbsp non-fat Greek yogurt (or sour cream)
  • salt and pepper to taste

Optional ingredients I’ve tried:

  • Cilantro (chopped up) - added a good lemony and more of a sunny vacation-y flavour to it
  • Chopped up jalapenos - little more zing is never a bad idea!

Pour the beans + corn into a bowl.

item.title: Dad's Creamy Cucumber Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 large cucumbers, peeled and thinly sliced
  • 1 sweet onion, thinly sliced
  • 1 tablespoon sea salt

Dressing

  • 1 ½ cups mayonnaise, or more to taste
  • 2 tablespoons vinegar
  • 1 tablespoon white sugar
  • 1 teaspoon dried dill weed
  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon ground black pepper

Instructions

  1. Mix cucumbers, onion, and salt together in a bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and let sit for 15 to 30 minutes.
  2. Turn cucumber mixture into a colander set over a bowl or in a sink. Let drain, stirring occasionally, until most of the liquid and salt has drained, 15 to 30 minutes. Transfer drained cucumber mixture to a large bowl.
  3. Whisk mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar, dill, garlic powder, and pepper for dressing together in a bowl until smooth. Pour over cucumber mixture and stir until coated.
  4. Pour over cucumber mixture and stir until coated.
  5. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours before serving.

Billy’s Notes:  Found this one on the internet and it was really good.  I know I’d tried a few of these but this was easily the best one I’ve tried.

item.title: Creamy Cucumber Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 long English cucumbers
  • Optional: 1/3 cup of sliced onion
  • 1/2 cup of sour cream (or plain Greek yogurt)
  • 3 Tablespoons of Mayonnaise
  • 1/4 Tsp of fresh dill (chopped)
  • 3 Tablespoons of white vinegar
  • 1/2 Teaspoon of white sugar
  • Salt to Taste

Instructions

  1. Peel the cucumbers and cut in half lengthwise. Scrape out the seeds if desired and slice into ¼″ slices.
  2. Combine all dressing ingredients and toss with cucumbers.
  3. Refrigerate 1 hour before serving.

Credit for this recipe goes to Spendwithpennies.com:

item.title: Big Mac Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 16 ounces of ground beef
  • 6 cups Romaine lettuce
  • 1/4 cup red onion, diced
  • 2 dill pickle spears, chopped
  • grate a bit of cheddar cheese and toss it in there too
  • Thousand Island Dressing
  • Salt and Pepper to taste (I didn’t use this)

Instructions

  1. Brown ground beef until cooked through.
  2. Chop Romaine lettuce into individual bowls.
  3. Chop red onion, pickles, and tomatoes and set aside.
  4. Pout Thousand Island Dressing on the lettuce as you desire.
  5. Mix the lettuce and dressing until evenly coated.
  6. Add ground beef and chopped vegetables to the lettuce and dressing and mix until evenly coated.
  7. Salt & Pepper to taste.

Billy’s Notes: I tweaked this a bit compared to the recipe linked below. Big Macs don’t have tomatoes and I grated some cheese on mine as well.

item.title: Sweet Chili Heat Corn Salad (Sandra)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 an orange pepper
  • 1/2 a red pepper
  • 1/3 a yellow pepper
  • 1 can of corn
  • 1/4 cup of shredded cheese (optional)
  • 2 cups crushed Sweet Chili Heat Doritos
  • 2 Tbsp of mayonnaise

Instructions

Mix everything but the Doritos ahead of time.  When you’re ready to serve it, mix in the Doritos.

item.title: Pasta Salad (Nandini)
item.description:
Ingredients

Dressing:

  • 1/4 cup oil
  • 1/4 cup vinegar
  • 2 1/2 tbsp sugar
  • 1/4 concentrated orange juice
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 tsp crushed red pepper
  • 1/2 tsp basil
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Other stuff:

  • Whatever pasta you want
  • crushed feta cheese
  • grapes (red & green)
  • red and green peppers
  • olives
  • celery

Instructions

  1. Mix all that stuff up.  That’s your dressing.
  2. Cook choice of pasta, drain, and add the dressing.
  3. Add the other stuff cut up however you want, however much you want, because I don’t have these kinds of details.  You can blame Heather if it tastes bad.  She wrote down the initial recipe.
